title: Partial image encryption support
body:
While https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/732 brought in image encryption support in skopeo, this PR adds the support for partial image encryption. 

User will be able to select which layer of the image they want to encrypt instead of encrypting an entire image. This is very useful from the layer reusability point of view. e.g. User can take a standard `ubuntu:18:04` image and add their custom layer which they will like to protect with the encryption. 
 When that image is saved in the snapshotter of the runtime, the layers that aren't encrypted end up getting reused which results in better disk space utilization as well as faster downloads (only have to download encrypted layers if the rest of the layers are already present in the snapshotter)

Signed-off-by: Harshal Patil <harshal.patil@in.ibm.com>

comments.body:
